items.title: Thai police probe weapons cache at Chinese man's home
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thai police are investigating a vast arsenal of military-grade weapons – including assault rifles, explosives, grenades and anti-personnel mines – found at the home of a Chinese man, authorities said today. Two M16 assault rifles, hundreds of rounds of ammunition, Russian landmines, projectiles, gasoline and nearly five kilos (11 pounds) of explosives were found at the home of Sun Mingchen, 31, near the beach resort of Pattaya. There were no clear links to a planned attack, Chonburi province's public relations department said in a statement, but local police chief Pongphan Wongmanithet told reporters at the scene they were "investigating security implications."

items.title: Bintulu Port handover process at final documentation stage - Loke
items.summary: KOTA SAMARAHAN: The process of handing over Bintulu Port to the Sarawak State Government is expected to be completed within the next few months after all key issues have been finalised, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said a final meeting with Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas will be held soon to finalise the matters. He said the enforcement of the authority's dissolution and the official handover to the state government remain subject to the signing of the final agreement.

items.title: Umno's 80th anniversary a launchpad for the next 20 years, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno's 80th anniversary is not merely a commemoration of its 1946 origins but a milestone to shape the party's direction for the next two decades, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said the party's eight decades in the political landscape should serve as a period of reflection to strengthen strategic planning and produce capable leaders to ensure continuity as Umno approaches its centenary in 2046. "Umno experiences a crisis almost every 10 years, and 2018 was a crisis of declining public confidence.

items.title: Aeronas sues Cape Manufacturing for RM279.5mil over aircraft deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cape Manufacturing (M) Sdn Bhd (Cape), a subsidiary of Cape EMS Bhd, is facing a RM279.5 million lawsuit by Aeronas Aerokraf Sdn Bhd (Aeronas) for breach of contract. The civil suit filed by Aeronas against Cape is based on an alleged breach of agreement, whereby Aeronas claims that Cape failed to fulfil its contractual obligations under the agreement dated March 9, 2025, namely to carry out a capital injection and provide a loan to Aeronas amounting to more than RM50 million. Aeronas owns the licensing and exclusive rights from Mael Aircraft Corporation to manufacture and commercialise the Model BA-42 twin-engine turboprop six-passenger aircraft.

items.title: Zahid: Umno, BN comfortable in Unity Government, focus remains on people
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) are comfortable working with all component parties in the Unity Government as they share common objectives in serving the people, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said the current cooperation is the result of a strong understanding among government parties to ensure the public is not burdened by political polemics. Zahid, who is also BN chairman, said all parties in the Unity Government have agreed to set aside political differences to ensure the effective implementation of the 15th General Election (GE15) manifestos.

items.title: Top Sri Lankan Buddhist monk arrested over alleged sex abuse
items.summary: COLOMBO: Sri Lankan authorities arrested a senior Buddhist monk on Saturday for the alleged sexual abuse of an underage girl, police said, marking the highest-profile case involving clergy in the country. The crime is alleged to have taken place at a highly venerated temple in Anuradhapura, around 200 kilometres (125 miles) north of Colombo, where Hemarathana is the chief priest. Authorities added that the victim's mother has also been arrested for aiding and abetting the monk.

items.title: Fuel fraud risks carry severe penalties under Customs Act, warns lawyer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — False declarations, manipulated documents, or undocumented fuel movements could expose companies and individuals to severe penalties under the Customs Act 1967, cautioned Admiralty and Maritime lawyer Datuk J. Shamesh. He was commenting on recent concerns raised by an industry player regarding revenue leakage and documentation gaps in Malaysia’s petroleum supply chain. “In addition, Section 135 deals with the fraudulent evasion of customs duties or prohibition controls, while Section 138 concerns offences linked to the creation or use of false documents.

items.title: Nanta hints at leaving Kapit to make way for younger blood
items.summary: KUCHING: Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi has hinted that he will not defend his Kapit parliamentary seat in the next general election, signaling an end to nearly 30 years of service at the federal level. Nanta, 68, said the move is intended to make way for younger leaders to continue the party's struggle and bring fresh leadership to the constituency. Nanta, who is also Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) secretary-general, did not rule out the possibility of continuing his political career at the state level if given the opportunity and the blessing of the party leadership.

items.title: Bersatu suspension: Ketereh MP Khlir Mohd Nor 'not surprised' by decision
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Ketereh member of parliament Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor, who was among five Bersatu MPs suspended by the party yesterday, said he accepted the decision with an open heart. "I was not surprised by yesterday's decision," he said today. Yesterday, Bersatu suspended five MPs for two party election terms over alleged violations of the party constitution and code of ethics.

items.title: Malaysia’s airspace stays resilient amid rising regional flight traffic, says CAAM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — Malaysia’s airspace operations remain resilient in handling high volumes of regional air traffic, underscoring the country’s role as a key aviation connectivity hub in Southeast Asia, according to the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM). In a statement today, CAAM said it continues to strengthen airspace efficiency within the Kuala Lumpur Flight Information Region (KL FIR) and Kota Kinabalu Flight Information Region (KK FIR) amid evolving global airspace dynamics and increasing operational complexity. CAAM said key corridors such as Kuala Lumpur-Singapore, Jakarta-Kuala Lumpur and Kota Kinabalu-Kuala Lumpur continue to record high flight frequencies.

items.title: Drugs seized from Jordanian man and wife traced to 'Abe'
items.summary: TUMPAT: Drugs found in the possession of a Jordanian man and his wife in a recent raid were supplied by an individual known as 'Abe', who is believed to be linked to a Thailand-based drug trafficking syndicate. General Operations Force Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the syndicate had also instructed the group to hand over the drugs to a man known only as "Lee". "Investigations showed the drugs were obtained from a dealer in Sungai Golok known as 'Abe'," he said.

items.title: Schools reach out to Canvas hackers as breach hits US classrooms
items.summary: DETROIT: Some schools and universities whose students' data was stolen by a cybercriminal hacking group as part of an April breach of the educational tool Canvas have reached out to the hackers to prevent their data from being released, a source familiar with the matter said on Friday. ShinyHunters, a hacking group with a string of data theft and extortion campaigns targeting major global companies, said in a May 3 post on its website that it had stolen roughly 6.65 terabytes of Canvas data related to nearly 9,000 schools worldwide that included student names, email addresses and private messages between students, teachers and other staff. Instructure pulled Canvas, Canvas Beta and Canvas Test offline a short time later, but restored access to Canvas four hours later.

items.title: Fire Dept marks first locally bred K9 detection dog with Labrador pup 'Brave'
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Fire and Rescue Department has achieved a milestone with the birth of its first locally bred K9 unit detection dog. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the male Labrador puppy, named Brave, was born on April 5 at the department's K9 Unit Operational Training Centre in Bertam, Penang. Nga said the successful local breeding could significantly reduce costs, as importing a trained detection dog from Birmingham in the United Kingdom typically costs about RM150,000 per animal.

items.title: Kedah govt to seek Sultan’s consent over fate of two Bersatu-sacked reps
items.summary: ALOR STAR, May 9 — The Kedah government will refer the positions of two state assemblymen — one a state executive councillor and the other a deputy speaker — sacked by Bersatu yesterday to the Sultan of Kedah, Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, New Straits Times (NST) reported. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said any decision involving Suka Menanti assemblyman Dzowahir Ab Ghani, who is state agriculture, plantation and transportation committee chairman, and Sungai Tiang assemblyman Abdul Razak Khamis, who is a deputy state assembly speaker, must follow due process and receive the consent of the Kedah ruler. “They are Independent assemblymen who support the state government.

items.title: Communications Ministry steps in to train small traders at Melaka central market
items.summary: MELAKA, May 9 — The Communications Ministry will channel allocations to the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) and the Madani Community to carry out targeted outreach programmes for traders at the Melaka Historic City Council (MBMB) Central Market in Bachang here. Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said his visit today found that many traders had requested classes on online sales, as well as assistance with applications for the Madani Subsidy Assistance Programme (BUDI) Individual for diesel. Also present were Communications Ministry deputy secretary-general (Strategic Communications and Creative Industry) Nik Kamaruzaman Nik Husin and Information Department director-general Julina Johan.

items.title: Army deploys drones, ATVs and scramblers along Malaysia’s border ahead of Aidiladha to curb smuggling
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 9 — The Malaysian Army (TDM) is tightening security along the country’s borders to curb smuggling and cross-border crimes ahead of the Aidiladha celebrations later this month. Army chief General Datuk Azhan Md Othman said the force remains on constant alert to ensure the continued security and sovereignty of the nation’s borders. Earlier, Azhan said the army would continue strengthening the deployment of personnel and assets along the country’s borders to ensure border security remains safeguarded and under control.

items.title: Need help getting around? Rapid KL rolls out ‘Ask Me’ helpers to guide commuters, starting at Pasar Seni
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — Rapid KL has introduced the “Ask Me” customer assistance initiative at the Pasar Seni Bus Hub as the first location for its implementation in an effort to enhance the public transport travel experience through faster, clearer and more accessible information delivery. Rapid KL said in a statement today that the initiative was introduced to help passengers obtain travel-related assistance more easily, particularly at bus hubs with high commuter traffic and multiple route connections. “Passengers can obtain information on bus routes, stop locations, route changes, travel planning as well as travel pass products to help facilitate their daily journeys.

items.title: Rapid KL launches 'Ask Me' initiative for better travel experience
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Rapid KL has launched the "Ask Me" customer assistance initiative at the Pasar Seni Bus Hub to provide commuters with faster and more accessible travel information. Rapid KL said in a statement today that the initiative helps passengers obtain travel assistance more easily, particularly at high-traffic hubs with multiple route connections. Rapid Bus acting chief executive officer Ku Jamil Zakaria said the initiative reflects Rapid KL's commitment to making public transport more responsive and reliable.

items.title: ‘Brave’ joins Bomba’s K9 unit as Putrajaya pumps RM2.4m to expand tracker dog force
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 9 — The K9 Tracker Dog Unit of the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has welcomed a new ‘member’ after its Labrador, Lottie, gave birth to a male puppy on March 18. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the puppy, born to the dog under the care of fire officer Nelson Karupak, has been named ‘Brave’. Also present were Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Aiman Athirah Sabu and JBPM director-general Datuk Nor Hisham Mohammad.

items.title: 1,000-year-old Quran in Narathiwat among region's valuable Islamic artefacts
items.summary: Weighing about 50 kilogrammes, the manuscript was produced during the Abbasid era using 346 sheets made from animal skin and carbon-based ink derived from natural materials, making it one of the rare and valuable ancient Islamic artefacts preserved in the region. Museum officer Nik Ilham Nik Yusup said the museum's oldest artefact was produced with exceptional craftsmanship using hundreds of processed animal skins to create durable sheets for Quranic writing. Written entirely by hand in ancient Kufi script, the manuscript used organic carbon ink produced from natural sources.

items.title: Army ramps up border enforcement to curb cross-border crimes, leakages
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The army has ramped up enforcement along the country's frontiers to clamp down on cross-border crimes and curb leakages. Army chief General Datuk Azhan Md Othman said heightened controls and enforcement measures were being implemented in line with the current security situation and annual trends observed. Previously, in his speech during the Army Day celebrations last month, Azhan said the army will continue to strengthen the deployment of its personnel and assets along the country's borders to ensure national border security remains safeguarded and under control.

items.title: Zahid says BN cooperation in unity govt driven by shared goals, not political complacency
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) are comfortable working with all component parties in the Unity Government, as they share common objectives, particularly in serving the interests of the people, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Ahmad Zahid, who is also the BN chairman, said that in the interest of the people, all parties in the Unity Government have agreed to set aside differences and political considerations to ensure the effective implementation of every agenda outlined in the 15th General Election (GE15) manifesto. He also said that 47 per cent of the manifestos of parties in the Unity Government had common elements, thereby forming a strong foundation for existing cooperation.

items.title: Retiree loses RM900,000 life savings to YouTube investment scam
items.summary: The case was highlighted by Kepong Member of Parliament Lim Lip Eng, who shared the victim's account as a warning against increasingly sophisticated financial fraud targeting retirees. According to Lim, the victim was initially lured by professionally produced videos featuring purported "investment experts" promising high and easy returns. At first, small returns were even credited into the man's account," Lim said.

items.title: Long working hours among doctors require wider healthcare reforms, says MMA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's healthcare system must undergo broader reforms to address excessive working hours and improve conditions for doctors and healthcare workers, the Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) said. Its president Datuk Dr Thirunavukarasu Rajoo said reports of house officers working up to 85 hours a week were deeply concerning and posed risks not only to doctors' welfare but also to patient safety. Thirunavukarasu said the MMA welcomed the reminder by Health director-general Datuk Dr Mahathar Abu Wahab to hospital directors regarding the flexible working hours system for house officers.

items.title: Taiping council ready to cooperate with authorities over elephant transfer claims
items.summary: TAIPING: The Taiping Municipal Council (MPT) is ready to cooperate with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) following social media allegations surrounding an international elephant conservation programme. The programme involved the transfer of three elephants, Dara, Amoi, and Kelat, from Zoo Taiping & Night Safari (ZTNS) to the Tennoji Zoo in Japan. He said the programme involving elephants Dara, Amoi and Kelat complied with conservation guidelines and all legal requirements under the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 (Act 716).

items.title: Boy, 12, dies after being trapped under concrete block in Pasir Puteh
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH, May 9 — A 12-year-old boy was killed after being crushed by a concrete block while playing in front of his home in Kampung Telaga Papan, Semerak, here yesterday, Harian Metro reported. Pasir Puteh police chief Superintendent Saari Yaacob said a family member later alerted others after the two children were found to have fallen into a sand pit and were discovered trapped under a concrete block. Earlier, Jejak Jalanan activist Tengku Mohd Harith Hakimin Tengku Abdul Azis said the victim’s family were recipients of monthly aid from the non-governmental organisation.

items.title: Three rescued, 10 houses submerged in Sungai Siput flash floods
items.summary: IPOH: More than 10 houses in Kampung Lasah, Sungai Siput, were submerged in metre-deep floodwaters early today following four hours of continuous heavy rain. The Sungai Siput Civil Defence Force (APM) operations control centre said several surrounding roads, including those in Kampung Chenein, Kampung Jong, and Kampung Bawong, were also inundated. Conditions are currently under control," the statement said.

items.title: Burden or duty? Debate grows over proposed filial responsibility law, with calls for govt support
items.summary: KUCHING, May 9 — Members of the public have shared their views following reports that the government is considering ‘filial responsibility law’ to legally require children to care for their ageing parents. Robert Ukin, 40, said he would support the law, describing it as ‘a rightful duty of children towards their parents’. “As children, we need to ensure that we care for our parents, including their daily needs, medical care and wellbeing,” he told The Borneo Post here.

items.title: Umno opens 'Rumah Bangsa' doors to sacked, suspended Bersatu leaders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno is ready to accept leaders who were recently sacked or suspended by Bersatu under the Rumah Bangsa initiative. The party's secretary-general, Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, said Umno would welcome them unconditionally. When contacted, Asyraf said Umno would welcome back those who were once part of the party.

items.title: Trapped seafarers traumatised by Gulf fighting
items.summary: LONDON: Isolated and traumatised by drones and missiles, seafarers in the Gulf face grave mental suffering after more than two months stuck on board in the Middle East war, maritime charities warn. It's pretty scary," said Gavin Lim, head of the Crisis Response Network for the Sailors' Society, a UK-based seafarers' charity, who spoke with one crew whose vessel was hit. At least 11 seafarers have been killed, according to the International Maritime Organization.

items.title: Man swept away in Kepong found dead after 3-day search
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A three-day search for a man believed to have fallen into Sungai Keruh in Kepong ended in tragedy when his body was discovered yesterday in a retention pond in Taman Sri Segambut. The Kuala Lumpur Civil Defence Force said it received an emergency call regarding the incident at 5.27pm on Wednesday. The SAR operation took three days and covered Sungai Keruh, Sungai Gombak and Sungai Klang, involving a search radius of 16km.

items.title: Don't blame my wife, says father of boy crushed by concrete block
items.summary: Nor Ismail Nasir, 52, said his eldest son, Mohammad Yusof, had learning disabilities and was hyperactive. He was working in Pasir Mas when his wife, Nurul Anis Abdul Rahman, 36, called to say their son had been injured. Ismail said his son had never played near the 200kg concrete blocks before.

items.title: Injured wife still unaware husband was killed in home invasion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The family of an elderly man killed during a break-in robbery in Jalan Melayu, Ayer Tawar, yesterday has yet to tell his wife about his death. The victim's eldest son, identified only as Teh, said his mother, who was also attacked in the incident, remains hospitalised and is still unaware that her husband had died, China Press reported. He said the family feared the news could worsen her condition.

items.title: Proposed care law for ageing parents should consider support systems for families, says Sarawak minister
items.summary: KUCHING, May 9 — The proposed new law requiring children to care for their ageing parents must reflect the diverse realities across Malaysia, said State Minister of Women, Childhood and Community Wellbeing Development Sarawak Datuk Seri Fatimah Abdullah. On citizenship matters, the state minister said applications under Article 15A involving illegitimate children continued to make up the highest number of cases in Sarawak, with Kuching recording the most submissions, at nine, through JPN Kuching. Fatimah said 21 applications involved illegitimate children; those born before their parents’ marriage was properly registered.

items.title: Everything, everywhere, at once: Sabah’s Tamparuli sets record for largest simultaneous local elections with 94 villages
items.summary: TAMPARULI, May 9 — Tamparuli has made history after its Village Development and Security Committee (JKKK) election process was recognised by the Malaysia Book of Records (MBOR) for the “Most Villages in a Simultaneous Village Committee Election”. Tuaran MP and Tamparuli assemblyman Datuk Wilfred Madius Tangau said the recognition was awarded under that category involving the simultaneous election of village committees. He said the JKKK election involved a total of 94 villages across five mukim conducted simultaneously within the Tamparuli constituency, namely Mukim Tenghilan, Topokon, Mangkaladoi, Pantai and Mukim Tamparuli.

items.title: Indonesia resumes search for three hikers missing after Mount Dukono eruption
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesian authorities resumed search efforts on Saturday for three hikers missing following the eruption of Mount Dukono on Halmahera island, an official said. Mount Dukono, in North Maluku province, erupted on Friday at 7.41am (2241 GMT Thursday), spewing volcanic ash as high as 10km (6.2 miles) into the sky, the country's volcanology agency said. Saturday's search was still hampered by continued eruptions, Iwan said.

items.title: Million-ringgit cars, zero road tax: Offence leaves RTD 'baffled'
items.summary: Negri Sembilan Road Transport Department (RTD) director Jasyindar Singh Sidhu said 143 luxury vehicles were seized in the state in 2025 for the offence. Between January and April this year, another 45 luxury vehicles were seized for the same offence. "Among the vehicles seized in 2025 were seven Porsches, 11 Audis, 21 Toyota Alphards and Toyota Vellfires, 30 Mercedes-Benzes, 35 BMWs, and 39 other models.

items.title: Kedah exco sacked by Bersatu to continue duties for now, says MB
items.summary: ALOR STAR: The Kedah government will refer the positions of two assemblymen, one a state exco member and the other a deputy speaker, who were sacked by Bersatu yesterday, to the Sultan of Kedah, Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah. Dzowahir is the state agriculture, plantation and transportation committee chairman (exco), while Abdul Razak is a deputy state assembly speaker. He said both assemblymen continued to support the Perikatan Nasional-led state government.

items.title: Malaysia, Thailand team up for rail, energy and cross-border security
items.summary: CEBU, May 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim held a meeting with his Thai counterpart, Anutin Charnvirakul yesterday and expressed commitment to enhancing cooperation in areas including border development, energy security, rail connectivity, and logistics. Anwar said the bilateral meeting with Anutin took place in the spirit of friendship and growing mutual understanding between Malaysia and Thailand. — Bernama Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim (right) meets his Thai counterpart Anutin Charnvirakul (right) during the 48th Asean Summit and Related Meetings on May 8, 2026 in Cebu, the Philippines.

items.title: Anwar cautious over subsidy cuts, says Mat Sabu
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has made the welfare of the people a top priority, ensuring ministries do not arbitrarily remove subsidies for essential goods and food. Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said he and his officers had to meet with Anwar several times to discuss the decision to withdraw the chicken egg subsidy. He said the prime minister was initially hesitant to agree to the move due to concerns over potential price hikes for the staple item.

items.title: An undying love
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: It has been more than 787 days since Azreein Aziz lost his beloved wife, Nor Hazamiah Lokman, who passed away after suddenly falling ill and slipping into a coma. For Azreein, 50, life has never been the same. His wife's grave has become a place he returns to often - a quiet refuge where memories of love remain alive.

items.title: Lorry driver killed while crossing road after being hit by teen motorcyclist in Gua Musang
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, May 9 — An elderly lorry driver was killed after he was believed to have been hit by a motorcycle ridden by a 17-year-old while crossing a road in Gua Musang on Friday night, Sinar Harian reported. In the 8.30pm incident at Kilometre 41 of the Gua Musang–Kuala Krai road, the victim, Mat Zaki Jusoh, 62, died at the scene. Gua Musang police chief Superintendent Sik Choon Foo said preliminary investigations found that the accident occurred when the teenage motorcyclist was travelling from Paloh towards Gua Musang.

items.title: Bilateral ties with Cambodia to focus on security and job scam fight, says Anwar
items.summary: CEBU, May 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia and Cambodia are committed to intensifying cooperation in various sectors, including trade, halal, higher education, and strengthening regional security governance, particularly in combating cross-border crime and job scam activities. Both leaders also expressed commitment to enhancing cooperation in higher education, halal, agriculture, and tourism sectors, he said, adding that Malaysia also welcomes more students and tourists from Cambodia to the country in line with the spirit of openness and Asean’s shared development. “Malaysia values its long-standing relationship with Cambodia, including growing people-to-people ties, economic cooperation, and efforts to expand strategic networks across various fields,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Malaysia, Vietnam deepen ties in AI, digital economy and STEM cooperation
items.summary: CEBU (Philippines), May 9 — Malaysia and Vietnam are committed to expanding cooperation in the digital economy and artificial intelligence (AI), as well as science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM), said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Both nations also agreed to broaden cooperation in food security, renewable energy, and more sustainable and progressive financial governance following Anwar’s meeting with Vietnamese Prime Minister Le Minh Hung here yesterday. “Malaysia also welcomes the signing of various strategic initiatives, including a memorandum of understanding (MoU) on the digital economy, as well as the implementation of cross-border energy projects that can support regional growth,” said Anwar in a Facebook post yesterday.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim witnesses Su-57E fighter jet demonstration by ‘Hero of Russia’ pilot in Moscow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, yesterday graced an aerial demonstration of the fifth-generation multi-role fighter jet, the Sukhoi Su-57E, piloted by renowned Russian test pilot and “Hero of Russia” recipient, Sergei Bogdan. The nearly seven-minute flight demonstration at Zhukovsky International Airport in Moscow showcased the modern military technological capabilities of the Russian Federation, including the advanced radar systems and the multi-role operational capacity of the fighter jet. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s Facebook page today, His Majesty was also given a briefing on the fighter jet and took the time to inspect the interior of the Su-57E once it landed following the aerial display.

items.title: Anwar to seek explanation from Amir Hamzah over Pahang allocation row, says economy must be considered
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will ask Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan to provide a comprehensive explanation on the federal government’s allocation for Pahang, amid renewed scrutiny over the state’s revenue matters, Bernama reported. The matter comes after the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, called for federal intervention on issues involving the state’s revenue, including the approval of river sand exports. Earlier today, Tengku Hassanal expressed disappointment over the federal government’s decision to ban river sand exports, saying it has affected Pahang’s revenue.

items.title: Four months, over 15,000 fake accounts: MCMC flags surge in impersonation of Malaysian royalty on social media platforms
items.summary: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that these fake accounts included those involving the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, and the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin. Fahmi added that this matter was presented to the Sultan of Selangor during an audience today to update His Royal Highness on the latest reports regarding the removal of fake accounts involving the Royal family. Fahmi also acknowledged the Sultan of Selangor’s concerns regarding various current issues circulating on social media and the methods used by authorities to address them.

items.title: 44 evacuated as flash floods hit Bandar Baharu
items.summary: Bandar Baharu Civil Defence Force officer Lieutenant Abdul Rahim Khairuddin said the figures, recorded as of 7.30am today, involve residents from Kampung Sungai Tengas. The affected villages are Kampung Sungai Tengas, Kampung Air Itam Dalam, Kampung Cherok Meranti, Kampung Paya Salak, and Kampung Batu Hampar. Rahim added that the water level of Sungai Terunsing in Kampung Sungai Tengas has reached the danger level.

items.title: Ex-lawyer liable for misappropriating RM415,000 from senior citizen property sale, court rules
items.summary: Judge Effandi Nazila Abdullah said Na Wee Jern, who ran the law firm Wee Jern & Partners, failed to remit the sale proceeds to the plaintiff despite receiving the sale proceeds of the apartment. "The court agrees with the plaintiff's submission that the defendant as the appointed solicitor, has breached fiduciary responsibility toward the plaintiff when the defendant failed to release 90 per cent of the sale proceeds to the plaintiff according to the terms of the said SPA," the judge said. The judge said the defendant had a fiduciary responsibility toward the plaintiff (Tan) because the defendant (Na) acted as the sole trustee who received and kept any sale proceeds on behalf of the Plaintiff in the sale and purchase transaction of the said Property.

items.title: UiTM should be a Royal University, says alumni group citing royal address and legacy
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 9 — The Alumni Federation of Universiti Teknologi Mara (PAUiTM) has proposed that the university be granted Royal University status, in recognition of its significant role and contributions to the nation since its establishment. PAUiTM said the proposal was in line with the royal address by the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, who is also UiTM Pro-Chancellor, during the First Session of the 104th UiTM Convocation Ceremony last Wednesday (May 6), in which she described the public university as a “Royal University”. PAUiTM also conveyed its gratitude for the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor’s address in conjunction with the UiTM Convocation Ceremony, which carried profound messages, values, and reminders for university staff and alumni.

items.title: Anwar backs Selangor Sultan’s concerns over pig farms, cites public health risks
items.summary: CEBU, May 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the concerns raised by Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah over pig farming in the state were justified due to repeated excesses that have affected public health. The Prime Minister stressed that, in principle, he did not agree with pig farms operating without strict regulation, particularly when located too close to residential areas. There must be strict controls, and the farms must be located far away from residential areas.

items.title: Landslide hits Kampung Bukit Sungai Putih in Hulu Langat, residents told to evacuate home for safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — Residents of a house were instructed to evacuate their home after a landslide hit Kampung Bukit Sungai Putih, Hulu Langat near here yesterday afternoon. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they received a call at 2.17 pm and dispatched a six-man team from the Ampang Fire and Rescue Station with one engine to the location of the landslide, which arrived five minutes later. “A landslide about 1.2 metres was found and the residents of a house were instructed to vacate their home as a precautionary measure,” he said in a statement last night.

items.title: Hamzah ‘Reset’ gathering triggers Bersatu purge as MPs and assemblymen face suspension, expulsion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — The Bersatu Disciplinary Board has expelled and suspended the membership of several elected representatives and party leaders, effective immediately. They are: Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz (Tanah Merah); Datuk Abdul Khalib Abdullah (Rompin); Nordin Ahmad Ismail (Lumut); Datuk Rosol Wahid (Hulu Terengganu); Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor (Ketereh) Also suspended are Ahmad Man (Adun for Kuala Sepetang) and Syed Lukman Hakim Syed Mohd Zain (Adun for Chenderoh). When contacted by Bernama, Nordin Ahmad Ismail confirmed receiving the suspension letter.

items.title: Kedah hit by flash floods after two-hour downpour, 17 homes affected in Bandar Baharu
items.summary: “Twelve homes are in Kampung Sungai Tengas, two in Kampung Air Itam Dalam and one each in Kampung Cherok Meranti, Kampung Paya Salak and Kampung Batu Hampar,” he said in a statement last night, adding that seven families are being housed at the relief centre at Dewan KEDA Sungai Tengas. Meanwhile, he said that the water level at Sungai Tengas was rising while Sungai Air Itam Dalam and Kampung Cherok Meranti were dropping. The water levels of Sungai Terunsing and Sungai Terap Dalam were at danger levels, while the water level of Sungai Punti was still normal, he added.

items.title: One year on, how Warisan KL has breathed new life back to Kuala Lumpur’s past — and what’s coming up next
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — When he launched Warisan KL in April 2025, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was aiming to revitalise the heart and soul of Kuala Lumpur. They are: Carcosa and Seri Negara The Kuala Lumpur Old Railway Station Dataran Merdeka heritage area, including Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad The Merdeka 118 precinct Petaling Street Masjid Jamek enclave Central Market Medan Pasar Dayabumi Complex & City Point Dayabumi Masjid Negara One year on, Malay Mail takes a look at how much has been done and what is next. — Picture by Choo Choy May Under Khazanah Nasional’s stewardship, Seri Negara and Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad reopened to the public recently after a 10-month round-the-clock restoration exercise.

items.title: Six senior civil service appointments unveiled, government says move boosts efficiency and integrity in ministries
items.summary: Under the reshuffle, Ministry of Education (MOE) secretary-general Datuk Dr Aminuddin Hassim has been appointed secretary-general of the Ministry of Higher Education. Aminuddin’s post will be assumed by MOE deputy secretary-general (Planning and Development) Datuk Wan Hashim Wan Rahim. Also involved in the reshuffle are Datuk Dr Megat Sany Megat Ahmad Supian, who was appointed deputy secretary-general (Planning and Development) of MOE, and Datuk Bahria Mohd Tamil, who was appointed deputy secretary-general (Strategic Communications and Creative Industry) at the Ministry of Communications.

items.title: Honda City chase through Federal Highway ends in crash near Damansara Heights, police arrest two foreigners
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Police arrested two foreigners after a high-speed chase ended with their car crashing along Jalan Johor near Pavillion Damansara Heights early this morning. Petaling Jaya district police chief said that the police tried to stop a Honda City that was heading to the capital along the Federal Highway for inspection at 2.15am but the car sped away, leading to a high-speed chase along the SPRINT Highway, where the car skidded and crashed into a signboard at the U-turn as it was headed to Kuala Lumpur. “The police arrested the male driver and a woman passenger, both foreigners in their 20s at the crash, while another suspect managed to run away.

items.title: Crocodile attack suspected in Sarawak after missing fisherman’s remains discovered during Sungai Ridan search
items.summary: MIRI, May 8 — Body parts of an elderly man reported missing while fishing in Sungai Ridan, Marudi, yesterday have been found. “At 10.52am today, the search and rescue team discovered parts of the victim’s body, namely two legs, a head and a hand. “At 4pm, the search operation was fully concluded as the remaining body parts could not be found.

items.title: Deputy minister Sim stresses not all EVs will become expensive as CKD models remain accessible
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, May 8 — Claims that all electric vehicles (EVs) will become more expensive following the reimposition of import duties on completely built-up (CBU) EVs from July are inaccurate, said Deputy Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) Minister Sim Tze Tzin. He said consumers will still have access to a wide range of reasonably priced EVs through completely knocked-down (CKD) models. Sim said the CKD strategy would ensure EVs remain affordable within the RM100,000 to RM200,000 price range, thereby maintaining public access to EV technology.

items.title: PM Anwar reassures nation: Fuel supply steadier after enforcement and import push
items.summary: CEBU, May 8 — The government has assured that the nation’s petrol and diesel supply is more stable following the government’s proactive measures, particularly under the management of Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said reducing RON95 consumption, tightening enforcement to curb diesel leakages and smuggling, as well as importing fuel supplies, have helped strengthen the country’s supply security. He said Asean countries also discussed joint efforts to address supply shortages and ease rising cost pressures, including on food, fertiliser and transportation.
